title: Willow handoff v3 — machine block
description: Schema for the single fenced JSON block in a v3 handoff file. Written by code (Stop hook / recovery sweep); model-authored fields arrive via tool call and are serialized by code. Verification verdicts (verified/failed/unverifiable + checked_at) are boot-digest OUTPUT stamped at read time and are never stored in this block. See ADR-20260703-handoff-v3-claims-record.md.

session.description: Session label: UTC date + letter, e.g. 2026-07-03c

written_by.description: Provenance of the block itself. A crashed session yields stop_hook or recovery_sweep with empty narrative fields — still valid.

skeleton.description: Layer 1 — code-observed facts. All fields optional; empty arrays mean observed-none, absent fields mean not-observed.

claims.description: Layer 2 — open threads as typed claims. Replaces v2 Open Threads bullets.

next_bite.description: The single next action. Replaces v2 Q17. kind=prose allowed but discouraged — prefer a verifiable kind.

open_questions.description: Genuinely unresolved questions — as many as are real, NO fixed count, no padding (ADR-20260703: 17-Questions count retired, section kept). The next action does not belong here.

agreements.description: Decisions and constraints carried forward (narrative mirror of What We Agreed On).

summary.description: One-paragraph narrative summary. Empty string when no model was present.

id.description: Plain slug unique within this file, e.g. 'boot-digest-build'. No b17. Cross-file identity = same id + carried_from chain.

text.description: Terse English statement of the thread or action, <=200 chars preferred

verify.description: How the boot digest checks this claim at read time. Required for every kind except prose.

subject.description: What to check: branch name (branch_pushed), repo#number (pr_state), repo-relative path (file_exists), flag soil id (flag_open), SHA (sha_current)

expect.description: Expected value, kind-specific: pr_state -> open|merged|closed; file_exists -> true|false; flag_open -> true|false; branch_pushed -> true; sha_current -> true (subject SHA is an ancestor of origin/master HEAD... or equals booted head)

opened.description: UTC date the claim first appeared

carried_from.description: Session label of the handoff this claim was carried from, e.g. 2026-07-03b. Absent on first appearance. Chains make age visible; content is never re-copied without this stamp.

claimKind.description: v1 claim kinds. prose = explicitly unverifiable; the digest stamps it 'unverifiable' rather than guessing.
